framing_corrections.correction: Uganda is the genuinely NEW signal in this cycle (Ebola cross-border importation with urban Kampala cases), but a natural-disaster importation with a WHO-coordinated, non-obstructed state response is an EXOGENOUS overlay, not a scorable compassion-conduct downgrade. Treating an imported outbreak as a score reduction absent documented response failure would mis-attribute an exogenous shock; held at boundary 20.3.

An IPO S-1 filing can read as a major institutional event, but a change of corporate/ownership structure is not in itself a compassion-conduct change. It is net-neutral; the scorable axis remains conduct (safety practices, harms), which is unchanged this cycle.
